text: So far in the book, we have studied properties of numbers (whether a number is odd, positive, prime; whether one number is divisible by another) and operations on numbers (addition, greatest common divisor).
html: So far in the book, we have studied properties of numbers (whether a number is odd, positive, prime; whether one number is divisible by another) and operations on numbers (addition, greatest common divisor).
zh: 到目前为止，本书研究的是数的性质（一个数是否为奇数、正数、素数；一个数是否能被另一个数整除）以及数上的运算（加法、最大公因子）。

text: In this chapter we go up a level of abstraction, and study properties of and operations on functions. These new properties include: whether a function is injective , surjective , bijective ; whether one function is inverse to another; and the operation of composition .
html: In this chapter we go up a level of abstraction, and study properties of and operations on functions. These new properties include: whether a function is injective, surjective, bijective; whether one function is inverse to another; and the operation of composition.
zh: 在本章中，我们上升到更高的抽象层次，研究函数的性质以及函数上的运算。这些新性质包括：一个函数是否为单射、满射、双射；一个函数是否为另一个函数的逆；以及复合运算。

text: We also expand our horizon beyond the numeric types ( \(\mathbb{N}\) , \(\mathbb{Z}\) , \(\mathbb{Q}\) , \(\mathbb{R}\) ) which have formed the setting of the early part of the book. We now start to work with a broader range of types, including function types, finite inductive types, and product types.
html: We also expand our horizon beyond the numeric types (\(\mathbb{N}\), \(\mathbb{Z}\), \(\mathbb{Q}\), \(\mathbb{R}\)) which have formed the setting of the early part of the book. We now start to work with a broader range of types, including function types, finite inductive types, and product types.
zh: 我们还把视野扩展到数值类型（\(\mathbb{N}\)、\(\mathbb{Z}\)、\(\mathbb{Q}\)、\(\mathbb{R}\)）之外；这些数值类型构成了本书前半部分的语境。现在我们开始处理更广泛的类型，包括函数类型、有限归纳类型和积类型。

text: We have studied particular functions before. For example, the Fibonacci sequence from Example 6.3.3 is a function from \(\mathbb{N}\) to \(\mathbb{Z}\) : it takes in a natural number, for example 5, and gives out an integer, in this case 8 (the index-5 term of the sequence).
html: We have studied particular functions before. For example, the Fibonacci sequence from Example 6.3.3 is a function from \(\mathbb{N}\) to \(\mathbb{Z}\): it takes in a natural number, for example 5, and gives out an integer, in this case 8 (the index-5 term of the sequence).
zh: 我们以前已经研究过具体函数。例如，例 6.3.3 中的 Fibonacci 数列是从 \(\mathbb{N}\) 到 \(\mathbb{Z}\) 的函数：它输入一个自然数，例如 5，并输出一个整数，在这个例子中为 8（该数列第 5 项）。

text: The domain of a function is the type where it takes its input values, and the codomain of a function is the type where it takes its output values. For example, the domain of the Fibonacci sequence is \(\mathbb{N}\) and its codomain is \(\mathbb{Z}\) . The type of functions with domain \(\mathbb{N}\) and codomain \(\mathbb{Z}\) is denoted \(\mathbb{N}\to \mathbb{Z}\) . Lean will confirm for us that the Fibonacci sequence F has this type:
html: The domain of a function is the type where it takes its input values, and the codomain of a function is the type where it takes its output values. For example, the domain of the Fibonacci sequence is \(\mathbb{N}\) and its codomain is \(\mathbb{Z}\). The type of functions with domain \(\mathbb{N}\) and codomain \(\mathbb{Z}\) is denoted \(\mathbb{N}\to \mathbb{Z}\). Lean will confirm for us that the Fibonacci sequence F has this type:
zh: 函数的定义域是它取输入值所在的类型，陪域是它取输出值所在的类型。例如，Fibonacci 数列的定义域是 \(\mathbb{N}\)，陪域是 \(\mathbb{Z}\)。定义域为 \(\mathbb{N}\)、陪域为 \(\mathbb{Z}\) 的函数类型记为 \(\mathbb{N}\to \mathbb{Z}\)。Lean 会为我们确认 Fibonacci 数列 F 具有这个类型：

text: Now that we have the notation \(X\to Y\) for “the type of functions from \(X\) to \(Y\) ”, we can introduce properties of functions, in the same way that we have previously introduced properties (like “odd” and “prime”) of numbers. Here is our first one.
html: Now that we have the notation \(X\to Y\) for “the type of functions from \(X\) to \(Y\)”, we can introduce properties of functions, in the same way that we have previously introduced properties (like “odd” and “prime”) of numbers. Here is our first one.
zh: 既然我们已经有了表示“从 \(X\) 到 \(Y\) 的函数类型”的记号 \(X\to Y\)，就可以像以前给数引入性质（如“奇”“素”）一样，给函数引入性质。下面是第一个性质。

text: Definition
html: Definition
zh: 定义

text: A function \(f : X \to Y\) is injective , if for all \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) of type \(X\) , if \(f(x_1)=f(x_2)\) , then \(x_1=x_2\) .
html: A function \(f : X \to Y\) is injective, if for all \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) of type \(X\), if \(f(x_1)=f(x_2)\), then \(x_1=x_2\).

text: Problem
html: Problem
zh: 问题

text: Show that the function \(q:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) from Example 8.1.1 is injective.
html: Show that the function \(q:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) from Example 8.1.1 is injective.
zh: 证明例 8.1.1 中的函数 \(q:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\) 是单射。

text: Solution
html: Solution
zh: 解答

text: Let \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) be real numbers and suppose that \(q(x_1)=q(x_2)\) . Then \(x_1+1=x_2+1\) , so \(x_1=x_2\) .
html: Let \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) be real numbers and suppose that \(q(x_1)=q(x_2)\). Then \(x_1+1=x_2+1\), so \(x_1=x_2\).

text: Here is this solution in Lean. Note that after unfolding the definition “injective” using the command dsimp [Injective] , the goal state displays

zh: 下面是在 Lean 中的这个解答。注意，在用命令 dsimp [Injective] 展开“单射”的定义之后，目标状态显示

text: This is the “injective” definition specialized to the problem at hand.
html: This is the “injective” definition specialized to the problem at hand.
zh: 这正是“单射”定义特化到当前问题后的形式。

text: Problem
html: Problem
zh: 问题

text: Show that the function \(x \mapsto x ^ 2\) from \(\mathbb{R}\) to \(\mathbb{R}\) is not injective.
html: Show that the function \(x \mapsto x ^ 2\) from \(\mathbb{R}\) to \(\mathbb{R}\) is not injective.
zh: 证明从 \(\mathbb{R}\) 到 \(\mathbb{R}\) 的函数 \(x \mapsto x ^ 2\) 不是单射。

text: Solution
html: Solution
zh: 解答

text: We must show that there exist real numbers \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) , such that \(x_1{}^2=x_2{}^2\) and \(x_1\ne x_2\) . Indeed, -1 and 1 have these properties.
html: We must show that there exist real numbers \(x_1\) and \(x_2\), such that \(x_1{}^2=x_2{}^2\) and \(x_1\ne x_2\). Indeed, -1 and 1 have these properties.
zh: 我们必须证明存在实数 \(x_1\) 和 \(x_2\)，使得 \(x_1{}^2=x_2{}^2\) 且 \(x_1\ne x_2\)。确实，-1 和 1 具有这些性质。

text: Definition
html: Definition
zh: 定义

text: A function \(f : X \to Y\) is surjective , if for all \(y\) of type \(Y\) , there exists \(x\) of type \(X\) , such that \(f(x)=y\) .
html: A function \(f : X \to Y\) is surjective, if for all \(y\) of type \(Y\), there exists \(x\) of type \(X\), such that \(f(x)=y\).
zh: 函数 \(f : X \to Y\) 称为满射，如果对类型 \(Y\) 的每个 \(y\)，存在类型 \(X\) 的 \(x\)，使得 \(f(x)=y\)。

text: We have so far seen numeric types, like the integers \(\mathbb{Z}\) and the real numbers \(\mathbb{R}\) , and function types, like \(\mathbb{Z}\to \mathbb{R}\) (the functions from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to \(\mathbb{R}\) ).
html: We have so far seen numeric types, like the integers \(\mathbb{Z}\) and the real numbers \(\mathbb{R}\), and function types, like \(\mathbb{Z}\to \mathbb{R}\) (the functions from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to \(\mathbb{R}\)).
zh: 到目前为止，我们已经见过数值类型，例如整数 \(\mathbb{Z}\) 和实数 \(\mathbb{R}\)，以及函数类型，例如 \(\mathbb{Z}\to \mathbb{R}\)（从 \(\mathbb{Z}\) 到 \(\mathbb{R}\) 的函数）。

text: Another way to make a type is as a finite set of options. Finite types are useful for conceptual examples, because everything is explicit and checkable.
html: Another way to make a type is as a finite set of options. Finite types are useful for conceptual examples, because everything is explicit and checkable.
zh: 构造类型的另一种方式是把类型看作有限个选项的集合。有限类型对概念性例子很有用，因为一切都是显式且可检查的。

text: Here is how to define a function whose domain is a given finite inductive type.
html: Here is how to define a function whose domain is a given finite inductive type.
zh: 下面说明如何定义一个定义域为给定有限归纳类型的函数。

text: We can case-check on a variable a in a finite inductive type using the tactic cases .

text: Such proofs can become repetitive, and you may wish to use a trick we have seen before (for example Example 4.4.4 , Example 4.5.9 , Example 6.6.2 , Example 6.7.2 ): when a tactic such as cases generates many goals which can all be proved by the same tactic, you can write <;> to apply the tactic to all those goals.
html: Such proofs can become repetitive, and you may wish to use a trick we have seen before (for example Example 4.4.4, Example 4.5.9, Example 6.6.2, Example 6.7.2): when a tactic such as cases generates many goals which can all be proved by the same tactic, you can write <;> to apply the tactic to all those goals.
zh: 这样的证明可能变得重复；你也许想使用我们以前见过的技巧（例如例 4.4.4、例 4.5.9、例 6.6.2、例 6.7.2）：当某个策略（如 cases）生成许多都能由同一个策略证明的目标时，可以写 <;> 把该策略应用到所有这些目标上。

text: Theorem

text: A function \(f:X\to Y\) is bijective, if and only if for all \(y\) of type \(Y\) , there exists a unique \(x\) of type \(X\) , such that \(f(x)=y\) .

text: Proof
html: Proof
zh: 证明

text: First, suppose that \(f\) is bijective. Let \(y\) be of type \(Y\) . Since \(f\) is surjective, there exists \(x\) of type \(X\) , such that \(f(x)=y\) . We will show that this \(x\) is unique. Indeed, for any other \(x'\) with \(f(x')=y\) , we have that

text: Conversely, suppose that for all \(y\) of type \(Y\) , there exists a unique \(x\) of type \(X\) , such that \(f(x)=y\) . ( \(\star\) ) We must show that \(f\) is injective and surjective.
html: Conversely, suppose that for all \(y\) of type \(Y\), there exists a unique \(x\) of type \(X\), such that \(f(x)=y\). (\(\star\)) We must show that \(f\) is injective and surjective.
zh: 反过来，假设对类型 \(Y\) 的每个 \(y\)，存在唯一一个类型 \(X\) 的 \(x\)，使得 \(f(x)=y\)。（\(\star\)）我们必须证明 \(f\) 是单射且是满射。

text: For the injectivity, let \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) be of type \(X\) , and suppose that \(f(x_1)=f(x_2)\) . By ( \(\star\) ) applied with \(y=f(x_1)\) , there exists a unique \(x\) of type \(X\) for which \(f(x)=f(x_1)\) . So, by the uniqueness, \(x_1=x\) , and also, since \(f(x_2)=f(x_1)=f(x)\) , \(x_2=x\) . Combining these, \(x_1=x_2\) .

By (\(\star\)) applied with \(y=f(x_1)\), there exists a unique \(x\) of type \(X\) for which \(f(x)=f(x_1)\). So, by the uniqueness, \(x_1=x\), and also, since \(f(x_2)=f(x_1)=f(x)\), \(x_2=x\). Combining these, \(x_1=x_2\).

text: Solution
html: Solution
zh: 解答

text: We must show that there exists a function \(f:\mathbb{N}\to \mathbb{N}\) , which is injective but not bijective.
html: We must show that there exists a function \(f:\mathbb{N}\to \mathbb{N}\), which is injective but not bijective.
zh: 我们必须证明存在一个函数 \(f:\mathbb{N}\to \mathbb{N}\)，它是单射但不是双射。

text: Indeed, consider the function \(f(n)=n+1\) . This function is injective, since for all natural numbers \(n_1\) and \(n_2\) , if \(n_1+1=n_2+1\) then \(n_1=n_2\) .
html: Indeed, consider the function \(f(n)=n+1\). This function is injective, since for all natural numbers \(n_1\) and \(n_2\), if \(n_1+1=n_2+1\) then \(n_1=n_2\).
zh: 确实，考虑函数 \(f(n)=n+1\)。这个函数是单射，因为对所有自然数 \(n_1\) 和 \(n_2\)，若 \(n_1+1=n_2+1\)，则 \(n_1=n_2\)。

text: However, this function is not surjective, and therefore not bijective. To see this, observe that for all natural numbers \(n\) , we have \(f(n)=n+1>0\) and therefore \(f(n)\ne 0\) .
html: However, this function is not surjective, and therefore not bijective. To see this, observe that for all natural numbers \(n\), we have \(f(n)=n+1>0\) and therefore \(f(n)\ne 0\).
zh: 然而，这个函数不是满射，因此不是双射。要看出这一点，注意对所有自然数 \(n\)，都有 \(f(n)=n+1>0\)，因此 \(f(n)\ne 0\)。

text: Consider the function \((x,y)\mapsto x + y\) from \(\mathbb{R}^2\) to \(\mathbb{R}\) . Show that this function is

text: Solution
html: Solution
zh: 解答

text: 1. We will show that there exist points \((x_1,y_1)\) and \((x_2,y_2)\) in \(\mathbb{R}^2\) such that \(x_1+y_1=x_2+y_2\) and \((x_1,y_1)\ne (x_2,y_2)\) . Indeed, consider the points \((0,0)\) and \((1,-1)\) . We have that \(0+0=1+-1\) and \((0,0)\ne (1,-1)\) .
html: 1. We will show that there exist points \((x_1,y_1)\) and \((x_2,y_2)\) in \(\mathbb{R}^2\) such that \(x_1+y_1=x_2+y_2\) and \((x_1,y_1)\ne (x_2,y_2)\). Indeed, consider the points \((0,0)\) and \((1,-1)\). We have that \(0+0=1+-1\) and \((0,0)\ne (1,-1)\).

text: 2. Let \(a\) be a real number. We must show that there exists a point \((x,y)\) in \(\mathbb{R}^2\) such that \(x+y=a\) . Indeed, \(a+0=a\) , so \((a,0)\) has this property.
html: 2. Let \(a\) be a real number. We must show that there exists a point \((x,y)\) in \(\mathbb{R}^2\) such that \(x+y=a\). Indeed, \(a+0=a\), so \((a,0)\) has this property.
zh: 2. 设 \(a\) 为实数。我们必须证明存在 \(\mathbb{R}^2\) 中的点 \((x,y)\)，使得 \(x+y=a\)。确实，\(a+0=a\)，所以 \((a,0)\) 具有这个性质。

text: Consider the function \((m,n)\mapsto 5m+8n\) from \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) to \(\mathbb{Z}\) . Show that this function is

text: Solution
html: Solution
zh: 解答

text: 1. We will show that there exist pairs \((m_1,n_1)\) and \((m_2,n_2)\) in \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) such that \(5m_1+8n_1=5m_2+8n_2\) and \((m_1,n_1)\ne (m_2,n_2)\) . Indeed, consider the pairs \((0,0)\) and \((8,-5)\) . We have that \(5\cdot 0+8\cdot 0=5\cdot 8+8\cdot -5\) and \((0,0)\ne (8,-5)\) .
html: 1. We will show that there exist pairs \((m_1,n_1)\) and \((m_2,n_2)\) in \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) such that \(5m_1+8n_1=5m_2+8n_2\) and \((m_1,n_1)\ne (m_2,n_2)\). Indeed, consider the pairs \((0,0)\) and \((8,-5)\). We have that \(5\cdot 0+8\cdot 0=5\cdot 8+8\cdot -5\) and \((0,0)\ne (8,-5)\).

text: 2. Let \(a\) be an integer. We must show that there exists a pair \((m,n)\) in \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) such that \(5m+8n=a\) . Indeed, \(5(-3a)+8(2a)=a\) , so \((-3a,2a)\) has this property.
html: 2. Let \(a\) be an integer. We must show that there exists a pair \((m,n)\) in \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) such that \(5m+8n=a\). Indeed, \(5(-3a)+8(2a)=a\), so \((-3a,2a)\) has this property.
zh: 2. 设 \(a\) 为整数。我们必须证明存在 \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) 中的数对 \((m,n)\)，使得 \(5m+8n=a\)。确实，\(5(-3a)+8(2a)=a\)，所以 \((-3a,2a)\) 具有这个性质。

text: Let \(f:X\to\mathbb{N}\) be a function, let \(x_0\) be of type \(X\) with \(f(x_0)=0\) , and let \(i:X\to X\) be a function such that, for all \(x\) , \(f(i(x))=f(x)+1\) . We record this theorem for future use under the name surjective_of_intertwining . example { f : X → ℕ } { x0 : X } ( h0 : f x0 = 0 ) { i : X → X } ( hi : ∀ x , f ( i x ) = f x + 1 ) : Surjective f := by sorry

• Consider the following functions \(a\) and \(b\) from the Humour type (see Example 8.3.6) to itself. Write down a function \(c\) from the Humour type to itself, so that \(b \circ a=c\).

    def a : Humour → Humour
  | melancholic => sanguine
  | choleric => choleric
  | phlegmatic => phlegmatic
  | sanguine => melancholic

def b : Humour → Humour
  | melancholic => phlegmatic
  | choleric => phlegmatic
  | phlegmatic => melancholic
  | sanguine => sanguine

def c : Humour → Humour
  | melancholic => sorry
  | choleric => sorry
  | phlegmatic => sorry
  | sanguine => sorry

text: Let \(m_1\) and \(m_2\) be integers and suppose that \(q(m_1)=q(m_2)\) . Then by definition \[(m_1+1,2-m_1)=(m_2+1,2-m_2),\] so \(m_1+1=m_2+1\) and \(2-m_1=2-m_2\) , so \(m_1=m_2\) .
html:
• Let \(m_1\) and \(m_2\) be integers and suppose that \(q(m_1)=q(m_2)\). Then by definition

    \[(m_1+1,2-m_1)=(m_2+1,2-m_2),\]

    so \(m_1+1=m_2+1\) and \(2-m_1=2-m_2\), so \(m_1=m_2\).

text: We will show that there exists \((a,b)\) in \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) such that for all integers \(m\) , \(q(m)\ne(a,b)\) . Indeed, we will show that \((0,1)\) has this property. Suppose that \(m\) were an integer with \(q(m)=(0,1)\) . Then by definition \[(m+1,2-m)=(0,1),\] so \(m+1=0\) and \(2-m=1\) , hence \[\begin{split}1&=(m+1)+(2-m)-2\\ &=0+1-2\\ &=-1,\end{split}\] contradiction.
html:
• We will show that there exists \((a,b)\) in \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) such that for all integers \(m\), \(q(m)\ne(a,b)\). Indeed, we will show that \((0,1)\) has this property. Suppose that \(m\) were an integer with \(q(m)=(0,1)\). Then by definition

    \[(m+1,2-m)=(0,1),\]

    so \(m+1=0\) and \(2-m=1\), hence

    \[\begin{split}1&=(m+1)+(2-m)-2\\ &=0+1-2\\ &=-1,\end{split}\]

    contradiction.
zh: 我们将证明存在 \(\mathbb{Z}^2\) 中的 \((a,b)\)，使得对所有整数 \(m\)，都有 \(q(m)\ne(a,b)\)。确实，我们证明 \((0,1)\) 具有这个性质。假设存在整数 \(m\) 满足 \(q(m)=(0,1)\)。则按定义有 \[(m+1,2-m)=(0,1),\] 所以 \(m+1=0\) 且 \(2-m=1\)，于是 \[\begin{split}1&=(m+1)+(2-m)-2\\ &=0+1-2\\ &=-1,\end{split}\] 矛盾。
